75→\tfor _, register := range g {\n    76→\t\tregister.RegisterGRPC(s)\n    77→\t}\n    78→}\n    79→\n    80→// GRPCServer configures the dependencies associated with the Flipt GRPC Service.\n    81→// It provides an entrypoint to start serving the gRPC stack (Run()).\n    82→// Along with a teardown function (Shutdown(ctx)).\n    83→type GRPCServer struct {\n    84→\t*grpc.Server\n    85→\n 418→// This methods blocks until Shutdown is called.\n   419→func (s *GRPCServer) Run() error {\n   420→\ts.logger.Debug(\"starting grpc server\")\n   421→\n   422→\treturn s.Serve(s.ln)\n   423→}\n   424→\n   425→// Shutdown tearsdown the entire gRPC stack including dependencies.\n   426→func (s *GRPCServer) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error {\n   427→\ts.logger.Info(\"shutting down GRPC server...\")\n   428→\n   429→\t// call in reverse order to emulate pop semantics of a stack\n   430→\tfor i := len(s.shutdownFuncs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n   431→\t\tif fn := s.shutdownFuncs[i]; fn != nil {\n   432→\t\t\tif err := fn(ctx); err != nil {\n   433→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   434→\t\t\t}\n   435→\t\t}\n   436→\t}\n   437→\n   438→\treturn nil\n   439→}\n   440→\n   441→type errFunc func(context.Context) error\n   442→\n   443→func (s *GRPCServer) onShutdown(fn errFunc) {\n   444→\ts.shutdownFuncs = append(s.shutdownFuncs, fn)\n   445→}\n   446→\n   447→var (\n   448→\tcacheOnce sync.Once\n   449→\tcacher    cache.Cacher\n   450→\tcacheFunc errFunc = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n   451→\tcacheErr  error\n   452→)\n   453→\n   454→func getCache(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) (cache.Cacher, errFunc, error) {\n   455→\tcacheOnce.Do(func() {\n   456→\t\tswitch cfg.Cache.Backend {\n   457→\t\tcase config.CacheMemory:\n   458→\t\t\tcacher = memory.NewCache(cfg.Cache)\n   459→\t\tcase config.CacheRedis:\n   460→\t\t\tvar tlsConfig *tls.Config\n   461→\t\t\tif cfg.Cache.Redis.RequireTLS {\n   462→\t\t\t\ttlsConfig = &tls.Config{MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS12}\n   463→\t\t\t}\n   464→\n   465→\t\t\trdb := goredis.NewClient(&goredis.Options{\n   466→\t\t\t\tAddr:            fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", cfg.Cache.Redis.Host, cfg.Cache.Redis.Port),\n   467→\t\t\t\tTLSConfig:       tlsConfig,\n   468→\t\t\t\tUsername:        cfg.Cache.Redis.Username,\n   469→\t\t\t\tPassword:        cfg.Cache.Redis.Password,\n   470→\t\t\t\tDB:              cfg.Cache.Redis.DB,\n   471→\t\t\t\tPoolSize:        cfg.Cache.Redis.PoolSize,\n   472→\t\t\t\tMinIdleConns:    cfg.Cache.Redis.MinIdleConn,\n   473→\t\t\t\tConnMaxIdleTime: cfg.Cache.Redis.ConnMaxIdleTime,\n   474→\t\t\t\tDialTimeout:     cfg.Cache.Redis.NetTimeout,\n   475→\t\t\t\tReadTimeout:     cfg.Cache.Redis.NetTimeout * 2,\n   476→\t\t\t\tWriteTimeout:    cfg.Cache.Redis.NetTimeout * 2,\n   477→\t\t\t\tPoolTimeout:     cfg.Cache.Redis.NetTimeout * 2,\n   478→\t\t\t})\n   479→\n   480→\t\t\tcacheFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n   481→\t\t\t\treturn rdb.Shutdown(ctx).Err()\n   482→\t\t\t}\n   483→\n   484→\t\t\tstatus := rdb.Ping(ctx)\n   485→\t\t\tif status == nil {\n   486→\t\t\t\tcacheErr = errors.New(\"connecting to redis: no status\")\n   487→\t\t\t\treturn\n   488→\t\t\t}\n   489→\n   490→\t\t\tif status.Err() != nil {\n   491→\t\t\t\tcacheErr = fmt.Errorf(\"connecting to redis: %w\", status.Err())\n   492→\t\t\t\treturn\n   493→\t\t\t}\n   494→\n   495→\t\t\tcacher = redis.NewCache(cfg.Cache, goredis_cache.New(&goredis_cache.Options{\n   496→\t\t\t\tRedis: rdb,\n   497→\t\t\t}))\n   498→\t\t}\n   499→\t})\n   500→\n   501→\treturn cacher, cacheFunc, cacheErr\n   502→}\n   503→\n   504→var (\n   505→\tdbOnce  sync.Once\n   506→\tdb      *sql.DB\n   507→\tbuilder sq.StatementBuilderType\n   508→\tdriver  fliptsql.Driver\n   509→\tdbFunc  errFunc = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n   510→\tdbErr   error\n   511→)\n   512→\n   513→func getDB(ctx context.Context, logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config, forceMigrate bool) (*sql.DB, sq.StatementBuilderType, fliptsql.Driver, errFunc, error) {\n   514→\tdbOnce.Do(func() {\n   515→\t\tmigrator, err := fliptsql.NewMigrator(*cfg, logger)\n   516→\t\tif err != nil {\n   517→\t\t\tdbErr = err\n   518→\t\t\treturn\n   519→\t\t}\n   520→\n   521→\t\tif err := migrator.Up(forceMigrate); err != nil {\n   522→\t\t\tmigrator.Close()\n   523→\t\t\tdbErr = err\n   524→\t\t\treturn\n   525→\t\t}\n   526→\n   527→\t\tmigrator.Close()\n   528→\n   529→\t\tdb, driver, err = fliptsql.Open(*cfg)\n   530→\t\tif err != nil {\n   531→\t\t\tdbErr = fmt.Errorf(\"opening db: %w\", err)\n   532→\t\t\treturn\n   533→\t\t}\n   534→\n   535→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"constructing builder\", zap.Bool(\"prepared_statements\", cfg.Database.PreparedStatementsEnabled))\n   536→\n   537→\t\tbuilder = fliptsql.BuilderFor(db, driver, cfg.Database.PreparedStatementsEnabled)\n   538→\n   539→\t\tdbFunc = func(context.Context) error {\n   540→\t\t\treturn db.Close()\n   541→\t\t}\n   542→\n   543→\t\tif driver == fliptsql.SQLite && cfg.Database.MaxOpenConn > 1 {\n   544→\t\t\tlogger.Warn(\"ignoring config.db.max_open_conn due to driver limitation (sqlite)\", zap.Int(\"attempted_max_conn\", cfg.Database.MaxOpenConn))\n   545→\t\t}\n   546→\n   547→\t\tif err := db.PingContext(ctx); err != nil {\n   548→\t\t\tdbErr = fmt.Errorf(\"pinging db: %w\", err)\n   549→\t\t}\n   550→\t})\n   551→\n   552→\treturn db, builder, driver, dbFunc, dbErr\n   553→}\n   554→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1150→\tassert.NotEmpty(t, body)\n  1151→}\n  1152→\n  1153→// readyYAMLIntoEnv parses the file provided at path as YAML.\n  1154→// It walks the keys and values and builds up a set of environment variables\n  1155→// compatible with viper's expectations for automatic env capability.\n  1156→func readYAMLIntoEnv(t *testing.T, path string) [][2]string {\n  1157→\tt.Helper()\n  1158→\n  1159→\tconfigFile, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n  1160→\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n  1161→\n  1162→\tvar config map[any]any\n  1163→\terr = yaml.Unmarshal(configFile, &config)\n  1164→\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n  1165→\n  1166→\treturn getEnvVars(\"flipt\", config)\n  1167→}\n  1168→\n  1169→func getEnvVars(prefix string, v map[any]any) (vals [][2]string) {\n  1170→\tfor key, value := range v {\n  1171→\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n  1172→\t\tcase map[any]any:\n  1173→\t\t\tvals = append(vals, getEnvVars(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%v\", prefix, key), v)...)\n  1174→\t\tcase []any:\n  1175→\t\t\tbuilder := strings.Builder{}\n  1176→\t\t\tfor i, s := range v {\n  1177→\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", s))\n  1178→\t\t\t\tif i < len(v)-1 {\n  1179→\t\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteByte(' ')\n  1180→\t\t\t\t}\n  1181→\t\t\t}\n  1182→\n  1183→\t\t\tvals = append(vals, [2]string{\n  1184→\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%s\", strings.ToUpper(prefix), strings.ToUpper(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", key))),\n  1185→\t\t\t\tbuilder.String(),\n  1186→\t\t\t})\n  1187→\t\tdefault:\n  1188→\t\t\tvals = append(vals, [2]string{\n  1189→\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%s\", strings.ToUpper(prefix), strings.ToUpper(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", key))),\n  1190→\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", value),\n  1191→\t\t\t})\n  1192→\t\t}\n  1193→\t}\n  1194→\n  1195→\treturn\n  1196→}\n  1197→\n  1198→func TestMarshalYAML(t *testing.T) {\n  1199→\ttests := []struct {\n  1200→\t\tname    string\n  1201→\t\tpath    string\n  1202→\t\twantErr error\n  1203→\t\tcfg     func() *Config\n  1204→\t}{\n {\n\tRegisterGRPC(*grpc.Server)\n}\n\ntype grpcRegisterers []grpcRegister\n\nfunc (g *grpcRegisterers) Add(r grpcRegister) {\n\t*g = append(*g, r)\n}\n\nfunc (g grpcRegisterers) RegisterGRPC(s *grpc.Server) {\n\tfor _, register := range g {\n\t\tregister.RegisterGRPC(s)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GRPCServer configures the dependencies associated with the Flipt GRPC Service.\n// It provides an entrypoint to start serving the gRPC stack (Run()).\n// Along with a teardown function (Shutdown(ctx)).\ntype GRPCServer struct {\n\t*grpc.Server\n\n\tlogger *zap.Logger\n\tcfg    *config.Config\n\tln     net.Listener\n\n\tshutdownFuncs []func(context.Context) error\n}\n\n// NewGRPCServer constructs the core Flipt gRPC service including its dependencies\n// (e.g. tracing, metrics, storage, migrations, caching and cleanup).\n// It returns an instance of *GRPCServer which callers can Run().\nfunc NewGRPCServer(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tlogger *zap.Logger,\n\tcfg *config.Config,\n\tinfo info.Flipt,\n\tforceMigrate bool,\n) (*GRPCServer, error) {\n\tlogger = logger.With(zap.String(\"server\", \"grpc\"))\n\tserver := &GRPCServer{\n\t\tlogger: logger,\n\t\tcfg:    cfg,\n\t}\n\n\tvar err error\n\tserver.ln, err = net.Listen(\"tcp\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", cfg.Server.Host, cfg.Server.GRPCPort))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating grpc listener: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tserver.onShutdown(func(context.Context) error {\n\t\treturn server.ln.Close()\n\t})\n\n\tvar store storage.Store\n\n\tswitch cfg.Storage.Type {\n\tcase \"\", config.DatabaseStorageType:\n\t\tdb, builder, driver, dbShutdown, err := getDB(ctx, logger, cfg, forceMigrate)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver.onShutdown(dbShutdown)\n\n\t\tswitch driver {\n\t\tcase fliptsql.SQLite, fliptsql.LibSQL:\n\t\t\tstore = sqlite.NewStore(db, builder, logger)\n\t\tcase fliptsql.Postgres, fliptsql.CockroachDB:\n\t\t\tstore = postgres.NewStore(db, builder, logger)\n\t\tcase fliptsql.MySQL:\n\t\t\tstore = mysql.NewStore(db, builder, logger)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported driver: %s\", driver)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"database driver configured\", zap.Stringer(\"driver\", driver))\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// otherwise, attempt to configure a declarative backend store\n\t\tstore, err = fsstore.NewStore(ctx, logger, cfg)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.Debug(\"store enabled\", zap.Stringer(\"store\", store))\n\n\t// Initialize tracingProvider regardless of configuration. No extraordinary resources\n\t// are consumed, or goroutines initialized until a SpanProcessor is registered.\n\ttracingProvider, err := tracing.NewProvider(ctx, info.Version, &cfg.Tracing)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tserver.onShutdown(func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\treturn tracingProvider.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t})\n\n\tif cfg.Tracing.Enabled {\n\t\texp, traceExpShutdown, err := tracing.GetExporter(ctx, &cfg.Tracing)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating tracing exporter: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver.onShutdown(traceExpShutdown)\n\n\t\ttracingProvider.RegisterSpanProcessor(tracesdk.NewBatchSpanProcessor(exp, tracesdk.WithBatchTimeout(1*time.Second)))\n\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"otel tracing enabled\", zap.String(\"exporter\", cfg.Tracing.Exporter.String()))\n\t}\n\n\t// base observability inteceptors\n\tinterceptors := []grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor{\n\t\tgrpc_recovery.UnaryServerInterceptor(grpc_recovery.WithRecoveryHandler(func(p interface{}) (err error) {\n\t\t\tlogger.Error(\"panic recovered\", zap.Any(\"panic\", p))\n\t\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.Internal, \"%v\", p)\n\t\t})),\n\t\tgrpc_ctxtags.UnaryServerInterceptor(),\n\t\tgrpc_zap.UnaryServerInterceptor(logger),\n\t\tgrpc_prometheus.UnaryServerInterceptor,\n\t\totelgrpc.UnaryServerInterceptor(),\n\t}\n\n\tvar cacher cache.Cacher\n\tif cfg.Cache.Enabled {\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\tcacheShutdown errFunc\n\t\t\terr           error\n\t\t)\n\t\tcacher, cacheShutdown, err = getCache(ctx, cfg)\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver.onShutdown(cacheShutdown)\n\n\t\tstore = storagecache.NewStore(store, cacher, logger)\n\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"cache enabled\", zap.Stringer(\"backend\", cacher))\n\t}\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tfliptsrv    = fliptserver.New(logger, store)\n\t\tmetasrv     = metadata.New(cfg, info)\n\t\tevalsrv     = evaluation.New(logger, store)\n\t\tevalDataSrv = evaluationdata.New(logger, store)\n\t\thealthsrv   = health.NewServer()\n\t)\n\n\tvar (\n\t\t// authOpts is a slice of options that will be passed to the authentication service.\n\t\t// it's initialized with the default option of skipping authentication for the health service which should never require authentication.\n\t\tauthOpts = []containers.Option[authmiddlewaregrpc.InterceptorOptions]{\n\t\t\tauthmiddlewaregrpc.WithServerSkipsAuthentication(healthsrv),\n\t\t}\n\t\tskipAuthIfExcluded = func(server any, excluded bool) {\n\t\t\tif excluded {\n\t\t\t\tauthOpts = append(authOpts, authmiddlewaregrpc.WithServerSkipsAuthentication(server))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t)\n\n\tskipAuthIfExcluded(fliptsrv, cfg.Authentication.Exclude.Management)\n\tskipAuthIfExcluded(evalsrv, cfg.Authentication.Exclude.Evaluation)\n\n\tvar checker *audit.Checker\n\n\t// We have to check if audit logging is enabled here for informing the authentication service that\n\t// the user would like to receive token:deleted events.\n\tif cfg.Audit.Enabled() {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tchecker, err = audit.NewChecker(cfg.Audit.Events)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar tokenDeletedEnabled bool\n\tif checker != nil {\n\t\ttokenDeletedEnabled = checker.Check(\"token:deleted\")\n\t}\n\n\tregister, authInterceptors, authShutdown, err := authenticationGRPC(\n\t\tctx,\n\t\tlogger,\n\t\tcfg,\n\t\tforceMigrate,\n\t\ttokenDeletedEnabled,\n\t\tauthOpts...,\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tserver.onShutdown(authShutdown)\n\n\tif cfg.Analytics.Enabled() {\n\t\tclient, err := clickhouse.New(logger, cfg, forceMigrate)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"connecting to clickhouse: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tanalyticssrv := analytics.New(logger, client)\n\t\tregister.Add(analyticssrv)\n\n\t\tanalyticsExporter := analytics.NewAnalyticsSinkSpanExporter(logger, client)\n\t\ttracingProvider.RegisterSpanProcessor(\n\t\t\ttracesdk.NewBatchSpanProcessor(\n\t\t\t\tanalyticsExporter,\n\t\t\t\ttracesdk.WithBatchTimeout(cfg.Analytics.Buffer.FlushPeriod)),\n\t\t)\n\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"analytics enabled\", zap.String(\"database\", client.String()), zap.String(\"flush_period\", cfg.Analytics.Buffer.FlushPeriod.String()))\n\n\t\tserver.onShutdown(func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\treturn analyticsExporter.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// initialize servers\n\tregister.Add(fliptsrv)\n\tregister.Add(metasrv)\n\tregister.Add(evalsrv)\n\tregister.Add(evalDataSrv)\n\n\t// forward internal gRPC logging to zap\n\tgrpcLogLevel, err := zapcore.ParseLevel(cfg.Log.GRPCLevel)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"parsing grpc log level (%q): %w\", cfg.Log.GRPCLevel, err)\n\t}\n\n\tgrpc_zap.ReplaceGrpcLoggerV2(logger.WithOptions(zap.IncreaseLevel(grpcLogLevel)))\n\n\t// add auth interceptors to the server\n\tinterceptors = append(interceptors,\n\t\tappend(authInterceptors,\n\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.ErrorUnaryInterceptor,\n\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.ValidationUnaryInterceptor,\n\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.FliptAcceptServerVersionUnaryInterceptor(logger),\n\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.EvaluationUnaryInterceptor(cfg.Analytics.Enabled()),\n\t\t)...,\n\t)\n\n\t// cache must come after auth interceptors\n\tif cfg.Cache.Enabled && cacher != nil {\n\t\tinterceptors = append(interceptors, middlewaregrpc.CacheUnaryInterceptor(cacher, logger))\n\t}\n\n\t// audit sinks configuration\n\tsinks := make([]audit.Sink, 0)\n\n\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.LogFile.Enabled {\n\t\tlogFileSink, err := logfile.NewSink(logger, cfg.Audit.Sinks.LogFile.File)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"opening file at path: %s\", cfg.Audit.Sinks.LogFile.File)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsinks = append(sinks, logFileSink)\n\t}\n\n\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.Enabled {\n\t\topts := []webhook.ClientOption{}\n\t\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration > 0 {\n\t\t\topts = append(opts, webhook.WithMaxBackoffDuration(cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar webhookSink audit.Sink\n\n\t\t// Enable basic webhook sink if URL is non-empty, otherwise enable template sink if the length of templates is greater\n\t\t// than 0 for the webhook.\n\t\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.URL != \"\" {\n\t\t\twebhookSink = webhook.NewSink(logger, webhook.NewWebhookClient(logger, cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.URL, cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.SigningSecret, opts...))\n\t\t} else if len(cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.Templates) > 0 {\n\t\t\tmaxBackoffDuration := 15 * time.Second\n\t\t\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tmaxBackoffDuration = cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\twebhookSink, err = template.NewSink(logger, cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.Templates, maxBackoffDuration)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsinks = append(sinks, webhookSink)\n\t}\n\n\t// based on audit sink configuration from the user, provision the audit sinks and add them to a slice,\n\t// and if the slice has a non-zero length, add the audit sink interceptor\n\tif len(sinks) > 0 {\n\t\tsse := audit.NewSinkSpanExporter(logger, sinks)\n\t\ttracingProvider.RegisterSpanProcessor(tracesdk.NewBatchSpanProcessor(sse, tracesdk.WithBatchTimeout(cfg.Audit.Buffer.FlushPeriod), tracesdk.WithMaxExportBatchSize(cfg.Audit.Buffer.Capacity)))\n\n\t\tinterceptors = append(interceptors, middlewaregrpc.AuditUnaryInterceptor(logger, checker))\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"audit sinks enabled\",\n\t\t\tzap.Stringers(\"sinks\", sinks),\n\t\t\tzap.Int(\"buffer capacity\", cfg.Audit.Buffer.Capacity),\n\t\t\tzap.String(\"flush period\", cfg.Audit.Buffer.FlushPeriod.String()),\n\t\t\tzap.Strings(\"events\", checker.Events()),\n\t\t)\n\n\t\tserver.onShutdown(func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\treturn sse.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tserver.onShutdown(func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\treturn tracingProvider.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t})\n\n\totel.SetTracerProvider(tracingProvider)\n\totel.SetTextMapPropagator(tracing.GetPropagators(&cfg.Tracing))\n\n\tgrpcOpts := unmarshalling, such that the function can emit\n// any errors derived from the resulting state of the configuration.\ntype Config struct {\n\tVersion        string []string\n}\n\n// Dir returns the default root directory for Flipt configuration\nfunc Dir() (string, error) {\n\tconfigDir, err := os.UserConfigDir()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"getting user config dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn filepath.Join(configDir, \"flipt\"), nil\n}\n\nfunc Load(path string) (*Result, error) {\n\tv := viper.New()\n\tv.SetEnvPrefix(EnvPrefix)\n\tv.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(\".\", \"_\"))\n\tv.AutomaticEnv()\n\n\tvar cfg *Config\n\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\tcfg = Default()\n\t} else {\n\t\tcfg = &Config{}\n\t\tfile, err := getConfigFile(context.Background(), path)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer file.Close()\n\t\tstat, err := file.Stat()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// reimplement logic from v.ReadInConfig()\n\t\tv.SetConfigFile(stat.Name())\n\t\text := filepath.Ext(stat.Name())\n\t\tif len(ext) > 1 {\n\t\t\text = ext[1:]\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !slices.Contains(viper.SupportedExts, ext) {\n\t\t\treturn nil, viper.UnsupportedConfigError(ext)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := v.ReadConfig(file); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tresult      = &Result{Config: cfg}\n\t\tdeprecators []deprecator\n\t\tdefaulters  []defaulter\n\t\tvalidators  []validator\n\t)\n\n\tf := func(field any) {\n\t\t// for-each deprecator implementing field we collect\n\t\t// them up and return them to be run before unmarshalling and before setting defaults.\n\t\tif deprecator, ok := field.(deprecator); ok {\n\t\t\tdeprecators = append(deprecators, deprecator)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// for-each defaulter implementing fields we invoke\n\t\t// setting any defaults during this prepare stage\n\t\t// on the supplied viper.\n\t\tif defaulter, ok := field.(defaulter); ok {\n\t\t\tdefaulters = append(defaulters, defaulter)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// for-each validator implementing field we collect\n\t\t// them up and return them to be validated after\n\t\t// unmarshalling.\n\t\tif validator, ok := field.(validator); ok {\n\t\t\tvalidators = append(validators, validator)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// invoke the field visitor on the root config firsts\n\troot := reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Interface()\n\tf(root)\n\n\t// these are reflected config top-level types for fields where\n\t// they have been marked as experimental and their associated\n\t// flag has enabled set to false.\n\tvar skippedTypes []reflect.Type\n\n\tval := reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Elem()\n\tfor i := 0; i < val.NumField(); i++ {\n\t\t// search for all expected env vars since Viper cannot\n\t\t// infer when doing Unmarshal + AutomaticEnv.\n\t\t// see: https://github.com/spf13/viper/issues/761\n\t\tstructField := val.Type().Field(i)\n\t\tif exp := val.Field(i).Addr().Interface()\n\t\tf(field)\n\t}\n\n\t// run any deprecations checks\n\tfor _, deprecator := range deprecators {\n\t\twarnings := deprecator.deprecations(v)\n\t\tfor _, warning := range warnings {\n\t\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, warning.Message())\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// run any defaulters\n\tfor _, defaulter := range defaulters {\n\t\tif err := defaulter.setDefaults(v); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := v.Unmarshal(cfg, viper.DecodeHook(\n\t\tmapstructure.ComposeDecodeHookFunc(\n\t\t\tappend(DecodeHooks, experimentalFieldSkipHookFunc(skippedTypes...))...,\n\t\t),\n\t)); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// run any validation steps\n\tfor _, validator := range validators {\n\t\tif err := validator.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// getConfigFile provides a file from different type of storage.\nfunc getConfigFile(ctx context.Context, path string) (fs.File, error) {\n\tu, err := url.Parse(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif slices.Contains(object.SupportedSchemes(), u.Scheme) {\n\t\tkey := strings.TrimPrefix(u.Path, \"/\")\n\t\tu.Path = \"\"\n\t\tbucket, err := object.OpenBucket(ctx, u)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer bucket.Close()\n\t\tbucket.SetIOFSCallback(func() (context.Context, *blob.ReaderOptions) { return ctx, nil })\n\t\treturn bucket.Open(key)\n\t}\n\n\t// assumes that the local file is used\n\tfile, err := os.Open(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn file, nil\n}\n\ntype defaulter interface {\n\tsetDefaults(v *viper.Viper) error\n}\n\ntype validator interface {\n\tvalidate() error\n}\n\ntype deprecator interface {\n\tdeprecations(v *viper.Viper) []deprecated\n}\n\n// fieldKey returns the name to be used when deriving a fields env var key.\n// If marked as squash the key will be the empty string.\n// Otherwise, it is derived from the lowercase name of the field.\nfunc fieldKey(field reflect.StructField) string {\n\tif tag := field.Tag.Get(\"mapstructure\"); tag != \"\" {\n\t\ttag, attr, ok := strings.Cut(tag, \",\")\n\t\tif !ok || attr == \"squash\" || attr == \"omitempty\" {\n\t\t\treturn tag\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.ToLower(field.Name)\n}\n\ntype envBinder interface {\n\tMustBindEnv(...string)\n}\n\n// bindEnvVars descends into the provided struct field binding any expected\n// environment variable keys it finds reflecting struct and field tags.\nfunc bindEnvVars(v envBinder, env, prefixes []string, typ reflect.Type) {\n\t// descend through pointers\n\tif typ.Kind() == reflect.Pointer {\n\t\ttyp = typ.Elem()\n\t}\n\n\tswitch typ.Kind() {\n\tcase reflect.Map:\n\t\t// recurse into bindEnvVars while signifying that the last\n\t\t// key was unbound using the wildcard \"*\".\n\t\tbindEnvVars(v, env, append(prefixes, wildcard), typ.Elem())\n\n\t\treturn\n\tcase reflect.Struct:\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < typ.NumField(); i++ {\n\t\t\tvar (\n\t\t\t\tstructField = typ.Field(i)\n\t\t\t\tkey         = fieldKey(structField)\n\t\t\t)\n\n\t\t\tbind(env, prefixes, key, func(prefixes []string) {\n\t\t\t\tbindEnvVars(v, env, prefixes, structField.Type)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tbind(env, prefixes, \"\", func(prefixes []string) {\n\t\tv.MustBindEnv(strings.Join(prefixes, \".\"))\n\t})\n}\n\nconst wildcard = \"*\"\n\nfunc appendIfNotEmpty(s []string, v ...string) []string {\n\tfor _, vs := range v {\n\t\tif vs != \"\" {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, vs)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// bind invokes the supplied function \"fn\" with each possible set of\n// prefixes for the next prefix (\"next\").\n// If the last prefix is \"*\" then we must search the current environment\n// for matching env vars to obtain the potential keys which populate\n// the unbound map keys.\nfunc bind(env, prefixes []string, next string, fn func([]string)) {\n\t// given the previous entry is non-existent or not the wildcard\n\tif len(prefixes) < 1 || prefixes[len(prefixes)-1] != wildcard {\n\t\tfn(appendIfNotEmpty(prefixes, next))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// drop the wildcard and derive all the possible keys from\n\t// existing environment variables.\n\tp := make([]string, len(prefixes)-1)\n\tcopy(p, prefixes[:len(prefixes)-1])\n\n\tvar (\n\t\t// makezero linter doesn't take note of subsequent copy\n\t\t// nolint https://github.com/ashanbrown/makezero/issues/12\n\t\tprefix = strings.ToUpper(strings.Join(append(p, \"\"), \"_\"))\n\t\tkeys   = strippedKeys(env, prefix, strings.ToUpper(next))\n\t)\n\n\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\tfn(appendIfNotEmpty(p, strings.ToLower(key), next))\n\t}\n}\n\n// strippedKeys returns a set of keys derived from a list of env var keys.\n// It starts by filtering and stripping each key with a matching prefix.\n// Given a child delimiter string is supplied it also trims the delimeter string\n// and any remaining characters after this suffix.\n//\n// e.g strippedKeys([\"A_B_C_D\", \"A_B_F_D\", \"A_B_E_D_G\"], \"A_B\", \"D\")\n// returns [\"c\", \"f\", \"e\"]\n//\n// It's purpose is to extract the parts of env vars which are likely\n// keys in an arbitrary map type.\nfunc strippedKeys(envs []string, prefix, delim string) (keys []string) {\n\tfor _, env := range envs {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, prefix) {\n\t\t\tenv = env[len(prefix):]\n\t\t\tif env == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif delim == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, env)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// cut the string on the child key and take the left hand component\n\t\t\tif left, _, ok := strings.Cut(env, \"_\"+delim); ok {\n\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, left)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn\n}\n\n// getFliptEnvs returns all environment variables which have FLIPT_\n// as a prefix. It also strips this prefix before appending them to the\n// resulting set.\nfunc getFliptEnvs() (envs []string) {\n\tconst prefix = \"FLIPT_\"\n\tfor _, e := range os.Environ() {\n\t\tkey, _, ok := strings.Cut(e, \"=\")\n\t\tif ok && strings.HasPrefix(key, prefix) {\n\t\t\t// strip FLIPT_ off env vars for convenience\n\t\t\tenvs = append(envs, key[len(prefix):])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn envs\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) validate() (err error) {\n\tif c.Version != \"\" {\n\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(c.Version) != Version {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid version: %s\", c.Version)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tout []byte\n\t\terr error\n\t)\n\n\tif r.Header.Get(\"Accept\") == \"application/json+pretty\" {\n\t\tout, err = json.MarshalIndent(c, \"\", \"  \")\n\t} else {\n\t\tout, err = json.Marshal(c)\n\t}\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif _, err = w.Write(out); err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n}\n\n// stringToEnumHookFunc returns a DecodeHookFunc that converts strings to a target enum\nfunc stringToEnumHookFunc[T constraints.Integer](mappings map[string]T) mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {\n\treturn func(\n\t\tf reflect.Type,\n\t\tt reflect.Type,\n\t\tdata interface{}) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif f.Kind() != reflect.String {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif t != reflect.TypeOf(T(0)) {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tenum := mappings[data.(string)]\n\n\t\treturn enum, nil\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc experimentalFieldSkipHookFunc(types ...reflect.Type) mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc
